codexCorrections.reason: AO weightings are assessment metadata, not mathematics knowledge nodes under the V4 ontology contract.

issues: Official point also requires constructing arguments through diagrams and sketching graphs, which are not represented in the mapped canonical nodes.
Mapped nodes cover logical deduction and precise symbolic language only.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping and add a diagram-construction or graph-sketching node if available.

issues: Official OT2.6 describes a problem-solving cycle (specify, collect, process, represent, interpret, repeat), which is not the same as the modelling cycle mapped.
No dedicated problem-solving-cycle node exists in the catalog.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain as the closest available node but annotate the semantic mismatch.

issues: Mapping is broader; the translation of a context into a model is spread across abstraction, simplification and modelling-assumptions nodes.
Mapped to the whole modelling-cycle node rather than a specific refine/evaluate node.
Single official point covers proof structure plus three named methods; mapping splits across multiple nodes and is broader.
Mapping includes both the parent indices node and its subnodes, which is redundant but collectively supported.
pointReviews.recommendation: Acceptable; consider using only the parent node or only the subnodes.

issues: Broad mapping across quadratic graphs, discriminant, completing the square, formula, factorisation and quadratics in a function.
Broad mapping across linear and quadratic inequalities, graphical regions and set notation.
Broad mapping across polynomial expansion, factorisation, division by a linear expression and the factor theorem.
Broad mapping across polynomial, reciprocal and asymptote graphs, intersections and direct proportion.
Mapping covers translations and stretches for the four official transformations; no reflection is required by the specification.
Broad mapping across circle equation forms, tangent, chord properties and angle in a semicircle.
Broad mapping across binomial expansion for positive integer powers, coefficients, Pascal's triangle and the binomial distribution model link.
Unit-circle definitions of sine, cosine and tangent for all arguments are not separately represented in the mapped nodes.
Sine/cosine rules, area formula and ambiguous case are well mapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; add a unit-circle or general-definition node if available.
Maintain mapping; the set of equation-type nodes covers the bullet well.
Maintain mapping; the split across exponential modelling and differentiation of exponentials is justified by the specification text.
Maintain mapping; change-of-base is subsumed within the exponential/logarithmic equations node.

issues: No supplied canonical node covers estimation of parameters using log-log or log-linear straight-line graphs.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a new canonical node for logarithmic graph parameter estimation, or map to a modelling/data node if one is added to the catalog.
Maintain mapping; the spread across derivative-concept and application nodes is reasonable for this multi-part bullet.

issues: The mapping omits CALC-INTE-APPL-BETW, which better represents the specification's explicit requirement to find 'the area of a region bounded by a curve and given straight lines'. The relation should be broader/acceptable rather than exact.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add CALC-INTE-APPL-BETW to the canonical-node set and change the relation to broader.
Maintain mapping; force-context applications are appropriately noted as having no dedicated pure-vector node.
Maintain mapping; the partial relation correctly reflects that only random sampling and bias have dedicated nodes.
Maintain mapping; frequency polygons are correctly noted as lacking a dedicated node.

issues: The correlation-does-not-imply-causation distinction is mapped only to the broad parent correlation node; no dedicated node captures this specific conceptual point.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add a dedicated node for causation versus correlation, or retain the weak mapping and flag it explicitly.

issues: Mapped to the general statistical-diagrams node STAT-DATA-REPR; the 'select or critique data presentation techniques' skill is broader than the node and is not explicitly represented.
Page reference is slightly off: section 2.4 appears on specification page 24, not p.25.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain the mapping but consider adding a statistical-reasoning or critique node, or annotate the unmapped evaluative aspect.

issues: Data cleaning (missing data, errors, outliers) is mapped to STAT-DATA-CLAS (classifying and tabulating data), which does not represent data cleaning.
No dedicated data-cleaning or outlier-handling node exists in the catalog.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create or locate a dedicated data-cleaning node; otherwise clearly annotate the mapping as weak and incomplete.

issues: Maps to PROB-DIST-CRV-PROB (probabilities from density functions), but the specification excludes formal knowledge of probability density functions; only the informal area-under-curve interpretation is required.
pointReviews.recommendation: Replace PROB-DIST-CRV-PROB with a broader probability-modelling node, or mark the pdf aspect as excluded.

issues: Discrete uniform distribution and calculation of mean/variance of discrete random variables are not separately represented; mapping is otherwise acceptable.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change; add a discrete-uniform node if the catalog is extended.

issues: Acceptance-region and p-value terminology are not mapped to dedicated nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change unless finer-grained hypothesis-testing nodes are added.

issues: No dedicated mechanics-quantities-and-units node exists; mapped to GEOM-MENS-UNIT (general units), NUM-RAT-RATE-SPED (speed/distance/time), and MECH-DYN-NEWT-MASS (mass/weight), which do not cover SI base/derived quantities or unit conversions.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create a mechanics-quantities-and-units node, or retain with a broad annotation that the mapping is weak.

issues: Newton's first law has no separate node and is covered only by the parent MECH-DYN-NEWT node; thrust/compression/resistance are not separately represented.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change; add a Newton's-first-law node and force-type nodes if the catalog is extended.

issues: Vector notation nodes VECT-BASIC-NOTN-UNIT and VECT-BASIC-NOTN-COLV are generic and do not specifically represent force-as-vector applications.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change; the scalar form is covered by the Newton's-second-law node.

issues: Smooth pulleys are not represented separately; they are subsumed under connected-particle and tension nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change unless a pulley-specific node is added.

issues: NUM-POW-INDX is a parent node covering all index types; it is broader than the three AS index laws listed.
Perpendicular-gradient condition is not mapped to a dedicated node; GEOM-COOR-LINE is a broad parent.
No dedicated node exists for the sum/linearity rule of integration.
The formula list includes a 3D magnitude formula while AS vector content is restricted to two dimensions; the formula itself is correctly mapped.
pointReviews.recommendation: No change for the formula point.

issues: Many set and interval notation symbols (subset, proper subset, empty set, universal set, intervals, etc.) lack dedicated canonical nodes, so the mapping is incomplete.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create missing notation nodes or accept the partial mapping with an explicit annotation.

issues: Many logical and comparison symbols have no canonical node; some mapped sequence/series notation nodes extend beyond AS content.
pointReviews.recommendation: Create missing notation nodes or reduce mappings to AS-relevant notation only.

issues: Only two canonical nodes are mapped for the ten operation/summation/product/factorial/modulus notation items in Appendix 2 section 3.
Basic arithmetic-operation notation, summation/product symbols, factorial and modulus have no mapped nodes even though suitable canonical nodes exist.
Relation is correctly marked partial, but coverage is too sparse for an 'exact' or 'acceptable' fit.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add mappings for the remaining operation symbols (e.g. basic arithmetic, modulus, factorial) or explicitly document them as unmapped notation-only symbols.

issues: Limit and increment notation (Appendix 2 section 4 items 4.5–4.6) are not mapped to canonical nodes.
Limit notation could reasonably map to CALC-DIFF-CONC-LIMT, but no suitable node exists for the increment symbols.
Relation is partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add CALC-DIFF-CONC-LIMT for limit notation and record the increment symbols as notation-only where no content node exists.

issues: All four exponential/logarithmic notation items (e, exp x, log_a x, ln x) map cleanly to the selected canonical nodes.
No explicit paper allocation is specified in the official text, and the candidate correctly records this as not-specified.
TRIG-BASE-RATR is a weak fit for the sin/cos/tan notation items because it denotes right-angled triangle ratios rather than general trigonometric function notation.
The reciprocal-function node (TRIG-FUNC-ADVN) covers cosec/sec/cot notation, but reciprocal trigonometric functions are not part of the 8MA0 subject content.
Relation is partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Replace TRIG-BASE-RATR with TRIG-FUNC-GRAP-SINE/COSN/TANG where appropriate, and flag reciprocal-function notation as A-Level-only content outside 8MA0.

issues: Displacement, velocity and acceleration vector symbols (Appendix 2 section 7 items 7.9–7.11) are not mapped.
VECT-SPAC-THRD-IJK covers the k unit vector, but three-dimensional vectors are outside the 8MA0 subject content (AS vectors are two-dimensional only).
pointReviews.recommendation: Map displacement/velocity/acceleration vector notation to appropriate kinematic or vector nodes, and remove or flag the 3D unit-vector node as beyond AS scope.

issues: Union and intersection of events notation (items 8.2–8.3) are not mapped despite available set-theory/probability-rule nodes.
PROB-DIST-NORM-MODE and PROB-DIST-NORM-STAN map normal-distribution notation, but the Normal distribution is not part of the 8MA0 subject content.
PROB-DRVD-DRVB-EXPV and PROB-DRVD-DRVB-VARI map expectation/variance notation, yet calculation of mean and variance of discrete random variables is excluded from 8MA0.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add NUM-SYS-SET-UNIN or PROB-COMB-RULE-MUTX for event union/intersection notation, and flag Normal-distribution and general discrete-RV mean/variance notation as beyond AS scope.

issues: SI unit symbols (kg, m, km, m s^-1 etc.) and time/gravity symbols are not mapped to canonical nodes.
MECH-FURT-RIGD-MOMT maps the Newton-metre notation, but moments of forces are not part of the 8MA0 subject content.
Relation is partial.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add suitable units/rate nodes or record unit/time symbols as convention-only, and flag the moment notation as beyond AS scope.

overallIssues.issue: OFFICIAL-8MA0-OT2.6 is mapped to the modelling cycle, but the official text describes a problem-solving cycle that is semantically distinct from modelling.
Some points have partial or acceptable mappings that omit minor aspects of the official requirement (e.g., diagram construction in OT1.1, unit-circle definitions in P1-5.1).
Many Paper 1 subpoints are mapped to several fine-grained canonical nodes; the mapping is collectively supported but the relation is broader than exact.

The supplied canonical node catalog lacks dedicated nodes for some AS-specific content, resulting in one unmapped point (logarithmic graph parameter estimation) and weak mappings for correlation versus causation and area between a curve and straight lines.
Several points rely on parent or adjacent nodes because the catalog lacks granularity for sampling methods, frequency polygons, and distinct population sections; the mappings remain acceptable.

The canonical node catalog lacks dedicated nodes for several AS skills, forcing broader or weak mappings: selecting/critiquing data presentation, cleaning data, mechanics SI units, Newton's first law, smooth pulleys, and set/interval notation.
One mapping includes content explicitly excluded from the AS specification (formal probability density function knowledge in 3.1b).
Page reference for section 2.4 is inaccurate; the content is on specification page 24 rather than p.25.

All reviewed points are drawn from Appendix 2 (Notation), a reference appendix rather than the numbered subject-content topics. The appendix has no explicit paper allocation and its items are notation conventions rather than standalone assessable content; mapping them to canonical content nodes conflates notation with topic coverage.

Several notation mappings attach symbols to canonical nodes that represent A-Level-only content (reciprocal trigonometric functions, Normal distribution, 3D vectors, moments of forces, general discrete random variable mean/variance), which is outside the 8MA0 subject content. Confirm whether these residual notation symbols should be retained for this qualification or excluded from the AS mapping.
Partial notation coverage leaves operation, set/event, limit and SI-unit symbols unmapped even though corresponding canonical nodes exist in the catalog.

The official point covering logarithmic graphs to estimate parameters in relationships of the form y = ax^n and y = kb^x (P1-6.6) has no canonical-node coverage and is recorded only as an unmapped partial representation; the ontology lacks the relevant leaf.
Assessment Objectives and paper-level weightings are intentionally excluded from the knowledge ontology and remain assessment metadata.
Inconsistent identifier naming for several Paper 2 sub-points: IDs such as OFFICIAL-Edexcel-8MA0-2.2a omit the 'P2' segment used elsewhere, risking cross-reference ambiguity.
Appendix 3 calculator requirements (mandatory calculator features and examination regulations) are not represented in the candidate index.
